message:
fix(fcp-registry): private SupplyChainEvidence verified flags (br-i5iv4)

crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:304-314 previously exposed
SupplyChainEvidence::tuf_verified and ::sigstore_verified as PUBLIC
booleans. br-pcmm8 changed the enforcement gate to consult those flags
instead of the trivially-bypassable evidence.is_some() check, but any
embedding crate could still write

    SupplyChainEvidence {
        tuf_verified: true,
        sigstore_verified: true,
        ..Default::default()
    }

without running a verifier adapter and still satisfy require_tuf /
require_sigstore. c046efde swapped a presence-only bypass for a
caller-asserted-bool bypass.

## Fix

Make the verified fields private. Add typed promotion paths:

  pub fn with_tuf_verification_result(self, &TufVerificationResult) -> Self
  pub fn with_sigstore_verification_result(self, &SigstoreVerificationResult) -> Self

Both only flip the corresponding flag when `result.verified == true`
so a failed verification cannot silently upgrade the evidence. Callers
obtain the result from a real TufVerifier::verify_target /
SigstoreVerifier::verify_bundle invocation.

Add pub getters tuf_verified() / sigstore_verified() (consumed by
enforce_supply_chain_verification_config).

Test-only escape hatches mark_tuf_verified_for_tests() /
mark_sigstore_verified_for_tests() are gated behind #[cfg(any(test,
feature = "test-mocks"))] so downstream release builds cannot reach
them.

Added SupplyChainEvidence::new() + builder methods
with_transparency_log_present(bool) and with_attestations(Vec) so
production call sites that synthesize unverified evidence can stay
compact without touching struct literals.

## Migrated call sites

Production (2):
  crates/fwc/src/supply_chain_cmd.rs:649 build_registry_supply_chain_evidence
  crates/fwc/src/main.rs:14763 build_registry_supply_chain_evidence
    (the two synthesize-from-CLI-attestation paths; both correctly
    stayed unverified).

Integration tests (4):
  crates/fcp-registry/tests/no_mock_integration.rs:552, 604, 655, 2231

Internal regression tests (2):
  tests::verify_bundle_rejects_unverified_tuf_evidence
  tests::verify_bundle_rejects_unverified_sigstore_evidence
    (pcmm8 regression — now use SupplyChainEvidence::new() and
    mark_*_for_tests()).

## New regression tests (2)

  supply_chain_evidence_refuses_failed_tuf_verification_result
  supply_chain_evidence_refuses_failed_sigstore_verification_result

Both pin the invariant that a VerificationResult with
verified == false MUST NOT promote the evidence flag even when
passed through the typed constructor.

## fwc/net_cmd.rs

Added DenyReason::IpNotAllowed handling to rule_id_for() +
suggestion_for() so the fwc net-policy advisor surfaces a structured
suggestion when the new gate (br-45tjy) rejects a resolved IP.

## Verified

  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline \
    -p fcp-registry --lib
  -> 264 passed (was 262 + 2 new); 98 pre-existing failures
     (base_manifest_toml fixture drift — unrelated, reproduces on
     HEAD without these changes).
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline \
    -p fcp-registry --test no_mock_integration --features test-mocks
  -> 88 passed; 0 failed.
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline \
    -p fcp-sandbox --lib
  -> 563 passed; 0 failed.

Closes flywheel_connectors-i5iv4.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-i4zm","title":"Revocation System (RevocationObject/Registry/Chain)","description":"## Overview

Implement the revocation system as specified in V2 spec §14.3. This is CRITICAL security infrastructure - the spec explicitly states: "Without revocation, 'compromised device' recovery is mostly imaginary."

Revocations are mesh objects distributed like any other object and MUST be enforced before use.

## Background & Justification

When a device is compromised or a key is leaked, the mesh MUST be able to:
1. Revoke capabilities issued to that device
2. Revoke the device's issuer key (can no longer mint tokens)
3. Remove the device from the mesh entirely
4. Revoke compromised connector binaries
5. Force zone key rotation

Without revocation, security incidents cannot be recovered from.

## Normative Types (V2 Spec §14.3)

### RevocationObject
```rust
pub struct RevocationObject {
    pub header: ObjectHeader,
    /// ObjectIds being revoked
    pub revoked: Vec<ObjectId>,
    /// Scope of revocation
    pub scope: RevocationScope,
    /// Human-readable reason
    pub reason: String,
    /// When revocation becomes effective
    pub effective_at: u64,
    /// Optional expiry (None = permanent)
    pub expires_at: Option<u64>,
    /// Owner signature (revocations MUST be owner-signed)
    pub signature: Signature,
}
```

### RevocationScope
```rust
pub enum RevocationScope {
    /// Revoke capability objects/tokens
    Capability,
    /// Revoke issuer keys (node can no longer mint tokens)
    IssuerKey,
    /// Revoke node attestation (removes device from mesh)
    NodeAttestation,
    /// Revoke zone key (forces rotation)
    ZoneKey,
    /// Revoke connector binary (supply chain response)
    ConnectorBinary,
}
```

### RevocationEvent (Chain Node)
```rust
/// Hash-linked chain for revocation freshness
pub struct RevocationEvent {
    pub header: ObjectHeader,
    pub revocation_object_id: ObjectId,
    pub prev: Option<ObjectId>,
    /// Monotonic chain sequence number (NORMATIVE)
    /// Enables O(1) freshness comparison: seq_a > seq_b ⟹ a is fresher than b
    pub seq: u64,
    pub occurred_at: u64,
    pub signature: Signature,
}
```

### RevocationHead (Checkpoint)
```rust
/// Enables freshness semantics: tokens bound to a rev_head, verifiers MUST
/// have revocation state >= that head before accepting the token
pub struct RevocationHead {
    pub header: ObjectHeader,
    pub zone_id: ZoneId,
    pub head_event: ObjectId,
    /// Sequence number of head_event (NORMATIVE)
    /// Enables O(1) freshness comparison without chain traversal
    pub head_seq: u64,
    pub epoch_id: EpochId,
    pub quorum_signatures: Vec<(TailscaleNodeId, Signature)>,
}
```

### RevocationRegistry
```rust
pub struct RevocationRegistry {
    revocations: HashMap<ObjectId, RevocationObject>,
    bloom_filter: BloomFilter,  // Fast negative lookup
    /// Latest revocation head known for this zone
    pub head: Option<ObjectId>,
}

impl RevocationRegistry {
    /// Check if object is revoked (MUST be called before use)
    pub fn is_revoked(&self, object_id: &ObjectId) -> bool {
        // Fast path: bloom filter definitely-not check
        if !self.bloom_filter.might_contain(object_id.as_bytes()) {
            return false;
        }
        self.revocations.contains_key(object_id)
    }
}
```

## Implementation Requirements

### 1. RevocationObject Creation and Signing
- Owner-only signing (revocations are critical operations)
- Support all RevocationScope variants
- Validate effective_at is in future or now
- Propagate via mesh gossip immediately

### 2. RevocationEvent Chain
- Hash-linked for integrity
- Monotonic seq for O(1) freshness comparison
- Each new revocation creates new chain node
- Support chain traversal for full audit

### 3. RevocationHead Quorum
- Requires quorum signatures from mesh nodes
- Binds capability tokens to revocation state
- Verifiers MUST check token.rev_head <= local.head
- Enables freshness guarantees

### 4. Bloom Filter Optimization
- Fast negative lookup for non-revoked objects
- Size tuned for expected revocation volume
- Rebuild on restart from persistent store

### 5. Revocation Enforcement Points

EVERY capability verification MUST include:
```rust
fn verify_capability_token(&self, token: &CapabilityToken) -> Result<()> {
    // MANDATORY: Check revocation before any other validation
    if self.revocation_registry.is_revoked(&token.object_id) {
        return Err(Error::Revoked);
    }
    
    // Check revocation freshness
    if let Some(token_rev_head) = token.rev_head {
        if !self.revocation_registry.is_at_least(token_rev_head)? {
            return Err(Error::RevocationStateStale);
        }
    }
    
    // Continue with normal verification...
}
```

### 6. Revocation Propagation
- High-priority gossip (revocations supersede other traffic)
- Persist to local store immediately
- Trigger re-evaluation of cached capability decisions

## Use Cases by Scope

### Capability Revocation
- Revoke specific capability tokens (e.g., leaked token)
- Immediate effect on all nodes

### IssuerKey Revocation  
- Node can no longer mint new tokens
- Existing tokens remain valid until they expire
- Used for semi-trusted devices

### NodeAttestation Revocation
- Complete device removal from mesh
- All capabilities issued TO and BY this node revoked
- Used for compromised devices

### ZoneKey Revocation
- Forces zone key rotation
- All zone members must re-enroll
- Used for catastrophic key compromise

### ConnectorBinary Revocation
- Specific binary hash revoked
- Connectors using that binary MUST stop
- Used for supply chain incidents

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] RevocationObject with all scope variants
- [ ] Owner-only signature validation
- [ ] RevocationEvent hash-linked chain
- [ ] Monotonic seq numbers for O(1) freshness
- [ ] RevocationHead with quorum signatures
- [ ] RevocationRegistry with bloom filter
- [ ] is_revoked() check at all enforcement points
- [ ] Revocation freshness comparison
- [ ] High-priority gossip propagation
- [ ] Persistent storage with crash recovery
- [ ] Audit events for all revocation operations

## Dependencies

- Core Types (ObjectHeader, ObjectId, etc.)
- Zone Architecture (zone-scoped revocations)
- Capability System (enforcement points)
- MeshNode (gossip propagation)
- Observability (audit events)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:44:25.848323653Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T09:47:20.691101782Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T09:47:20.691101782Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of 1n78.9 (enhanced with detailed V2 spec content)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-i4zm","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-i4zm","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-i4zm","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-i4zm","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-i58yx","title":"[REVIEW-P2][MEDIUM] fcp-oauth redirect_uri hardening rejects RFC-valid registered query components","description":"crates/fcp-oauth/src/oauth2.rs:321-327 now routes every configured redirect_uri through normalize_registered_redirect_uri(), and crates/fcp-oauth/src/redirect_allowlist.rs:116-145 rejects any registered redirect URI with a query string. OAuth 2.0 allows pre-registered query components on the redirection endpoint (RFC 6749 section 3.1.2), and providers commonly preserve them while appending code/state. Bug scenario: a standards-compliant client configured with https://client.example/callback?tenant=acme now fails OAuth2Client::new(), and even the callback allowlist helpers cannot represent such redirects because normalize_callback_redirect_uri() strips the entire query before comparison. Suggested fix: allow registered redirects to carry a fixed query component, preserve that component during normalization, and strip only the provider-added OAuth response parameters when validating callbacks.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:15:19Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:15:19Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-i5iv4","title":"[REVIEW-P2][HIGH] fcp-registry TUF/Sigstore gate still trusts caller-forgeable verification flags","description":"crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:304-314 makes SupplyChainEvidence a public struct with public tuf_verified/sigstore_verified booleans, crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:932-937 accepts Option<&SupplyChainEvidence> directly from the caller, and crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:1724-1739 now treats those booleans as the entire oracle for require_tuf/require_sigstore. Bug scenario: any embedding code can instantiate SupplyChainEvidence { tuf_verified: true, sigstore_verified: true, ..Default::default() } without running a verifier adapter and still satisfy the supposedly real verification gate, so c046efde replaces a presence-only bypass with a caller-asserted-bool bypass. Suggested fix: make the verified fields private and construct SupplyChainEvidence only from verifier result types (or perform TUF/Sigstore verification inside RegistryVerifier instead of trusting caller-supplied booleans).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:15:19Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:21:23.112828Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-i5iv4","title":"[REVIEW-P2][HIGH] fcp-registry TUF/Sigstore gate still trusts caller-forgeable verification flags","description":"crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:304-314 makes SupplyChainEvidence a public struct with public tuf_verified/sigstore_verified booleans, crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:932-937 accepts Option<&SupplyChainEvidence> directly from the caller, and crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:1724-1739 now treats those booleans as the entire oracle for require_tuf/require_sigstore. Bug scenario: any embedding code can instantiate SupplyChainEvidence { tuf_verified: true, sigstore_verified: true, ..Default::default() } without running a verifier adapter and still satisfy the supposedly real verification gate, so c046efde replaces a presence-only bypass with a caller-asserted-bool bypass. Suggested fix: make the verified fields private and construct SupplyChainEvidence only from verifier result types (or perform TUF/Sigstore verification inside RegistryVerifier instead of trusting caller-supplied booleans).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:15:19Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:31:56.079149Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:31:56.078898Z","close_reason":"Made SupplyChainEvidence::tuf_verified and sigstore_verified fields PRIVATE. Added constructor-only promotion paths: with_tuf_verification_result(&TufVerificationResult) and with_sigstore_verification_result(&SigstoreVerificationResult) that only flip the flag when result.verified == true. Added pub getters tuf_verified() / sigstore_verified(). Test-only escape hatch mark_tuf_verified_for_tests() / mark_sigstore_verified_for_tests() gated behind #[cfg(any(test, feature = 'test-mocks'))] so downstream release builds cannot reach them. Migrated all 2 production call sites (fwc/supply_chain_cmd.rs, fwc/main.rs) + 4 integration test sites (fcp-registry/tests/no_mock_integration.rs) + 2 internal pcmm8 tests to the new API. Added DenyReason::IpNotAllowed handling to fwc/net_cmd.rs rule_id_for + suggestion_for arms. 2 new regression tests supply_chain_evidence_refuses_failed_tuf_verification_result + supply_chain_evidence_refuses_failed_sigstore_verification_result confirm failed verification results do NOT promote the flag. Verified: 264 lib tests pass (was 262 + 2 new), 88 integration tests pass, 563 fcp-sandbox lib tests pass. Pre-existing base_manifest_toml fixture-drift failure (98 tests) is unrelated and reproduces on HEAD without these changes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-i7nk","title":"TEST: Error Taxonomy Unit Tests (FCP-XXXX Codes, Retry Semantics, AI Hints)","description":"# TEST: Error Taxonomy Unit Tests


## Goal
Validate the error taxonomy is stable, code-complete, and machine-actionable (retry semantics + hints) with golden vectors.

## Scope
Comprehensive tests for error code system and recovery hints.

## Test Categories

### Error Code Ranges
- [ ] FCP-1xxx (protocol errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-2xxx (auth/capability errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-3xxx (zone errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-4xxx (store/mesh errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-5xxx (connector errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-9xxx (internal errors) mapped correctly

### Error Structure
- [ ] All errors have stable `code` string
- [ ] All errors have human-readable `message`
- [ ] All errors have `retryable` flag
- [ ] Retryable errors have `retry_after_ms`
- [ ] Errors include `details` when applicable

### AI Recovery Hints
- [ ] Top 20 failure modes have ai_recovery_hint
- [ ] Hints are actionable and specific
- [ ] Hints don't leak secrets
- [ ] Hints are stable across versions

### Error Serialization
- [ ] Errors serialize to canonical CBOR
- [ ] Errors deserialize correctly
- [ ] Unknown error codes handled gracefully
- [ ] Forward compatibility for new codes

### Retry Semantics
- [ ] Retryable errors retry correctly
- [ ] Non-retryable errors fail immediately
- [ ] Retry budget is respected
- [ ] Exponential backoff applied

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/errors/`
- `error_protocol.cbor`
- `error_auth.cbor`
- `error_zone.cbor`
- `error_retryable.cbor`

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_error_taxonomy",
  "phase": "execute",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "error_code": "FCP-2101",
  "retryable": false,
  "ai_hint_present": true,
  "result": "pass"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All error codes are tested
- AI hints exist for common failures
- Error serialization is stable

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-icndt","title":"[MOR/C2.5] Establish quarterly claims-vs-reality debiasing reports process","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:33:13.740427Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T00:17:43.660396Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T00:17:43.659627Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["debiasing","documentation","process"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-icndt","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1h6ah","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:32.602054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-icndt","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1oj85","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:30.298064Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2638,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-icndt","author":"jemanuel","text":"WHAT: Establish a process for quarterly claims-vs-reality reports. Format: for each claim in prior report, record current status, evidence, and delta. Publish in docs/quarterly/. This normalizes incompleteness and prevents bi-annual shock when claims are found to be stale. WHY: L2 Debiasing recommended institutional debiasing practices. Multi-agent development amplifies completion bias (agents optimize for task completion metrics, not honest assessment). Without periodic reality checks, documentation drift compounds over time. WHERE: Create docs/quarterly/ directory. Create template. First report covers the MOR analysis findings reconciliation. ACCEPTANCE: (1) Template exists. (2) First quarterly report produced. (3) Process documented in AGENTS.md or CONTRIBUTING.md. ESTIMATED EFFORT: 1 day to set up template; 1 day per quarterly report. DEPENDS ON: C2.4 (claims audit) — first quarterly report is the claims audit output.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:18Z"},{"id":2669,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-icndt","author":"jemanuel","text":"C2.5 complete. Created docs/quarterly/TEMPLATE.md, produced inaugural 2026-Q2 report, added quarterly debiasing process to AGENTS.md. All 16 features verified baseline. Commit 70978239.","created_at":"2026-04-11T00:17:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ig2","title":"fcp.spotify: Listening History","description":"# fcp.spotify: Listening History (FCP2)

## Goal
Expose listening history surfaces for personalization and analytics:
- recently played
- top tracks/artists

This is sensitive personal data.

## Scope
- Get recently played (pagination / time window).
- Get top tracks/artists by time range.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `spotify.history.read` (sensitive)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Privacy hygiene:
  - never log track names, artist names, or listening timestamps
  - logs include only counts and correlation IDs

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `spotify.history.recently_played`
  - `spotify.history.top_tracks`
  - `spotify.history.top_artists`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - time-range selection

## Acceptance criteria
- History access is reliable and privacy-preserving.
